sourceDetail: TGB2604A TGI GB 2026 April (March 2025 - February 2026), population weight. Compass segments recreated in TGI as Boolean attitude audiences (see data-src/Compass_Audience_Settings.md); TGI names Adapters/Strivers map to the published Architects/Hustlers.
indexNote: Index = segment % divided by all-GB-adults %, x100. 100 = average; >120 or <80 is strongly differentiating. pct = % of segment. TGI audience shares differ from the survey segmentation sizes (deckSharePct); the deck is canonical on sizing.

lifestyle.label: “I prefer to buy products from companies who sponsor TV programmes”

“I find traditional news media is accurate”

full.note: Curated TGI cuts grouped for the explorer. Each block holds the strongest-differentiating attributes for that theme (by max |index-100| across the four segments), or the natural answer order for ordered demographics. pct = %Col (penetration within the audience). index = TGI index vs GB adults (100 = average). Parsed from State_of_Independance_TGI_Demographics/Lifestyle/Media_Consumption.xlsx, sheet "All Respondents"; segment columns ADAPTERS=architects, STRIVERS=hustlers, COASTERS=coasters, RETREATERS=retreaters. Nothing inferred.

lifestyle.label: Shopping & retail

architects.label: I buy new products before most of my friends
I pay extra to personalise products to suit my taste and style
People come to me for advice before buying new things
If I trust a brand, I buy it without looking at the price
I only buy products from a company with whose ethics I agree
I'm tempted to buy products I've seen advertised
I am often tempted to buy products/Brands advertised by influencers/Content creators

When I see a new brand I often buy it to see what it's like
I tend to go for premium rather than standard goods/Services
It's worth paying more for organic food

lifestyle.label: Money & finance

architects.label: I use my credit card mostly for business
A woman's place is in the home
I usually consult a professional financial adviser before deciding on financial matters
I prefer to buy brands which emphasise inclusion and diversity in their adverts
The government is doing enough to tackle climate change
I trust my government to be prepared for future global challenges
I would use apps to monitor how my children spend their money
I actively seek out companies and brands that offer ways to offset their impact on the environment (e.g. planting trees)
Rules are made to be broken
I trust the government to protect my privacy
I am prepared to invest my time and money to support companies that try to do good
With a credit card I can buy the sort of things I can't normally afford

architects.label: I am willing to pay to access content on magazine websites/Apps

Social media posts influence my political views
I pay more attention to online video ads than to other forms of online ads
Online advertising is more relevant to me than other advertising

I like to follow the magazines/Newspapers that I read on social media
Online adverts play a part in influencing the brands I buy

architects.label: I prefer vegan food and drink
I like to have barbecues as often as possible
I wouldn't let my children eat junk food
I prefer vegetarian food and drink
I often eat snacks while on the move rather than eating a proper meal
I don't have time to spend preparing and cooking food
I don't really care what ingredients are in a ready-Meal, as long as it is cheap and tastes good
I always think of the calories in what I eat
I always make sure I eat the recommended (5 a day) portions of fruit and vegetables everyday
I am always searching for and experimenting with new recipes
We usually have family meals at the weekend
I buy free range products whenever I can
